title: Lithocarpus caudatilimbus
intro: Lithocarpus caudatilimbus, also known as the Cat's Claw Fungus or Lithocarpus, is a plant species in the beech family (Fagaceae) that is native to Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Lithocarpus caudatilimbus include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which may help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. The bioactive constituents of this plant include lithocarpin, a polyphenolic compound, as well as other flavonoids and phenolic acids that are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lithocarpus caudatilimbus is often used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus caudatilimbus is classified within the family Fagaceae, which includes species of beech, oak, and related genera. This classification places Lithocarpus caudatilimbus among the deciduous and evergreen trees. Within the family Fagaceae, Lithocarpus caudatilimbus belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of evergreen trees native to Asia. The genus Lithocarpus is further divided into several species, with Lithocarpus caudatilimbus being one of them. Lithocarpus caudatilimbus is also classified as a species of stone or bur oak, characterized by its unique shape and characteristics.
